scope.authoritative_claim: Hugging Face Storage Buckets are mutable, non-versioned object storage suitable for checkpoints, logs, and intermediate artifacts; finished public artifacts remain in versioned Hub repositories.

topology.purpose: Ephemeral training checkpoints, optimizer state, resumable upload shards, candidate adapters, and build receipts before independent qualification.
Held-out evaluation outputs, benchmark run bundles, resource observations, red-team findings, and candidate qualification receipts separated from training objects.
Sanitized runtime traces, exact-served-model observations, restart evidence, bounded receipt batches, and noncanonical observability exports.

phase_1_stage_and_verify: Create a never-reused attempt prefix and upload payload objects without a COMPLETED marker.
Compute each payload SHA-256 and byte count locally before upload and retain that local inventory outside the mutable bucket.
Create a canonical-JSON manifest binding bucket qualified name, attempt id, object key, SHA-256, byte count, media type, producer identity, source revision, rights evidence, and created-at timestamp.
Sign the manifest as a DSSE PAE envelope using the bucket-stager attestation key; the release-approval key is never available to the stager.
Upload the signed manifest, then GET every payload object and the signed manifest from the bucket and recompute their SHA-256 and byte counts from readback bytes.
An independent readback verifier records a signed readback receipt only when every object, manifest digest, manifest signature, expected key, and expected byte count matches.
phase_2_complete: After a valid independent readback receipt exists, the completion-marker writer creates COMPLETED.json last under the same attempt control prefix.
COMPLETED.json binds the exact signed-manifest SHA-256, readback-receipt SHA-256, attempt id, bucket qualified name, verifier key id, verification time, and state COMPLETED.
The completion marker is signed by the independent verifier and uploaded with a conditional create operation that refuses overwrite.
The marker is read back and its bytes, signature, manifest reference, and SHA-256 are verified before the attempt becomes consumable.
consumer_acceptance_predicate: COMPLETED marker exists at the exact control key and has state COMPLETED.
Marker and manifest DSSE signatures validate against separate allowlisted role keys.
Marker manifest_sha256 equals the SHA-256 of the read-back signed manifest bytes.
Every declared object is present and its read-back SHA-256 and byte count match the signed manifest.
No undeclared object under the payload prefix is selected, loaded, executed, evaluated, published, or otherwise consumed.
The attempt id, bucket qualified name, rights evidence, source revision, and release candidate id match the requested candidate.
No mutable alias, filename, ETag, UI status, object presence, or bucket audit event substitutes for content hashes and signatures.

forbidden_content.description: Hugging Face tokens, S3-derived credentials, API keys, passwords, session cookies, SSH keys, or cloud credentials in payloads, metadata, logs, or manifests.
Private signing material, seed phrases, key-encryption passwords, or release-approval key material.
Unredacted personal, health, payment-card, biometric, or identity data.
Customer documents, prompts, completions, source code, logs, or attachments without explicit contractual storage and publication authority.
Training or evaluation content lacking row-level source, license or rights basis, revision, and provenance evidence.
Brain, Lake, formula, note, or graph rows that have not passed the signed training-admission and split-ledger contract.
Held-out evaluation prompts, reference answers, hidden labels, judge keys, or contamination-inducing derivatives in any training or checkpoint staging path.
Weights or adapters without exact base identity, immutable revision, license lineage, load receipt, and held-out qualification state.
Unscanned executable code, pickle payloads, arbitrary remote code, or binary plugins outside the approved artifact and sandbox policy.
Runtime traces, prompts, completions, stack traces, environment dumps, or OpenTelemetry exports before secret and sensitive-data scrubbing.
The only surviving or authoritative copy of a model, dataset, Space, release receipt, paper, DOI archive, or source release.
Use of latest, main, filename, ETag, bucket URL, or object presence as a substitute for an immutable revision and content digest.

authority_separation_constraints: A single credential or role may not both stage payload and write its COMPLETED marker.
A single credential or role may not both approve a release and publish that release.
Bucket ownership, object presence, a COMPLETED marker, or a valid staging signature never grants canonical release authority.
Canonical publication requires an independently signed completion receipt, a separately signed release approval, and exact digest readback from the versioned destination repository.
Release signing and approval private keys are never stored in, mounted from, or derived from a bucket.

primary_sources.claim: Buckets are mutable, non-versioned object storage intended for checkpoints, logs, and intermediate data; repositories remain the versioned publication surface.
Buckets support hf:// access, CLI sync, mounts, Jobs and Spaces volume mounts, and S3-compatible access patterns.
The S3-compatible gateway uses scoped credentials derived from Hugging Face user access tokens and applies only to Storage Buckets.
Bucket access uses Hub controls and operations can be represented in organization audit logs; this contract does not treat audit events as content-integrity or release proof.
